theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Energy (+2.70%) and Real Estate (+1.33%) led the market today, while Technology (-2.57%) and Industrials (-2.22%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -0.84% versus +0.93% for defensives and +0.08% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 -0.62%, Dow -0.39%, Nasdaq -1.33% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at -0.87%. The VIX rose 3.53% to 16.11, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 0.32% to $4,128.40, crude oil was little changed at $71.77, and bitcoin slipped 0.85% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
